title: Inverted logic in transfer validation allows any transfer
reason: The `require` predicate is wrong, but ERC777 still enforces `fromBalance >= amount` in `_move` and `_burn`, so over-balance transfers and burns revert rather than enabling theft.

title: Predictable farming fund address allows front-running
reason: The `_farmingFund` address being derivable from block data does not by itself give an attacker control over that address or a practical way to steal protocol funds.

title: UUPS test implementation has no upgrade authorization and is fully takeoverable
reason: The issue is confined to `contracts/test/Proxiable.sol`; without evidence that this test helper is part of a deployed production path, it is not a reportable protocol finding.

title: Fee calculation loses precision due to integer division order
reason: This is standard integer-rounding behavior in fee math and no realistic protocol-level loss, theft, or lockup path was substantiated.

title: Token burn occurs before state update
reason: If anything later reverted, the entire transaction would revert atomically and undo the burn; the cited mapping increment does not create an independent loss scenario.

title: External call without reentrancy guard
reason: `payFee` uses `transfer`, which forwards minimal gas, and there is no vulnerable post-call state to exploit; this is not a realistic reentrancy issue here.

title: Lack of access control on farming configuration
reason: The cited configuration is explicitly protected by `onlyOwner`, so this is expected governance authority rather than unintended privilege escalation.
